title: vitis cantoniensis
intro: Vitis cantoniensis, also known as the Chinese grapevine, is a species of grapevine native to China. It is a woody climber with medicinal properties, primarily used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat fever, sore throat, and digestive issues. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever and respiratory infections. Vitis cantoniensis is prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, tincture, and decoctions, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
